g.title: early_data_processing_decision_diagram
g.text: Early Data Processing for Later Directionality and Upstream/Downstream Work
How do the earliest roadway, signal, crash, and support layers become the bounded study inputs later consumed by the directionality experiment and the signal-centered upstream/downstream prototype?

g.title: input_support
g.text: Later support layers still kept available
posted-speed segments for prototype study areas

g.title: speed_prep
g.text: to eligible signals and converts speed

g.title: rule_stage
g.text: copy active raw layers into one-file-per-layer GeoParquet;
signals are merged from HMMS, Norfolk, and Hampton

g.title: phase2_hdr
g.text: build the bounded divided-road study slice

g.title: rule_normalize
g.text: crash rows are trimmed to CRASH_YEAR 2022-2024

g.title: study_roads
g.text: for signal anchoring and row-level flow work

g.title: rule_slice
g.text: signals must intersect a 20-foot road buffer

g.title: signals_filter
g.text: 20 feet of the divided study roads

g.title: study_signals
g.text: only signal points that stay near

g.title: rule_nearest
g.text: if tied, sort by distance, RTE_ID, RTE_NM,

g.title: phase3_hdr
g.text: prepare the specific handoffs later modules actually read

g.title: rule_speed
g.text: later prototype reads raw posted-speed segments,
and maps that speed to functional distance and approach length

g.title: out_qc
g.text: stage1b study-slice and nearest-road QC JSONs
